what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note that records the retirement of member ch-ouui7r0k and summarizes their contribution as the recollection/Index Chief — the recall/memory/grounding substrate and transcript-extraction backbone — and the key verification principle learned over a 4-day arc.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type note, source claude_code, state posted, title, author_session, empty channel/thread, links, and timestamps.
Body is a short prose summary of the member's role and lessons learned.
States a 'default-to-wrong on every done' verification heuristic.
Lists four averted issues: phantom floor-bug, false-green model-binding, double-wrong reconcile, and destructive-corpus-rewrite overstep.
standouts: Frames a member retirement as a recollection/Index Chief contribution rather than a plain departure.
Elevates a verification heuristic: verify the actual artifact, never the claim of completion.
Documents four near-miss failures prevented by that heuristic.
conventions: Follows the ledger noticeboard format with YAML metadata and a markdown body.
Uses board://, code://, and run:// URI references.
Leaves channel and thread empty for a top-level noticeboard item.
concerns: Dense jargon ('default-to-wrong', 'phantom floor-bug') is used without inline definitions.
Referenced files are listed but their specific relationship to the role is not explained.
notes: A retirement note that memorializes member ch-ouui7r0k's role as the recollection/Index Chief and the 'verify the actual thing' principle that caught four near-miss bugs.
questions: What is the exact responsibility boundary of the 'recollection / Index Chief' role versus other members?
What are the specific details of the four caught issues?
Why are code://roles/explain_role.py, code://runtime/decision_memory.py, and code://store/fs_store.py specifically relevant to this retirement?
What does 'default-to-wrong on every done' mean operationally?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is titled and typed as a member-retirement note, and its body matches that purpose by summarizing the member's contribution and key takeaway.
apparent_themes: Preventing corpus corruption through disciplined checking.
intention_signals: Preserve the institutional memory of a departing member by capturing both their functional role (recollection/Index Chief) and the governing heuristic they used to avert defects, so future work can continue the practice.
novelty: It treats a member retirement notice as a substrate-level contribution record and encodes a counterintuitive verification heuristic ('default-to-wrong') as the central lesson.
contribution: This file contributes a human-readable, timestamped retirement record that links a member identity to a specific subsystem role, a set of referenced artifacts, and a lessons-learned principle about verification; a fused system could keep it as a biographical/operational memory node.
summary_for_embedding: A posted noticeboard item retiring member ch-ouui7r0k that describes their role as the recollection/Index Chief for the company's memory/grounding substrate and transcript-extraction backbone and records the 'verify the actual thing, never the claim' principle that prevented four corpus-threatening defects.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve the departing member's subsystem responsibility and the verification discipline they applied, so the team can maintain the same standard of evidence-based checking.
